contest.title: 2024-2025 ICPC, NERC, Southern and Volga Russian Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Preferably Teams)

sources.notes: Read the complete current English statement, examples, and limits: n <= 200000, 0 <= v_i,r_i <= 10^6, 2 seconds, 512 MB. For every k, avg_k is the average over all k-subsets of max(min v_i,min r_i), modulo 998244353.
Read problem F in the official ICPC mirror statement booklet (PDF page 8 of 19), including the modular-fraction output definition.

Read the complete official F solution section (PDF pages 120-140). It uses the integer tail-sum identity, inclusion-exclusion on the two threshold events, m_i=min(v_i,r_i), a k-independent coefficient sequence, and one FFT/NTT binomial transform for O(n log n).

Actually opened and read the full Luogu article. It independently derives F(k)=sum_e(C(A_e,k)+C(B_e,k)-C(C_e,k)) for threshold counts A_e,B_e,C_e and evaluates the binomial transforms with NTT in O(V+n log n).

Read the full 2038F section and code. It derives E(S)=min_S v+min_S r-min_S min(v_i,r_i), independently sorts the three arrays, applies sum_{i>=k}x_i C(i-1,k-1) for subset minima, and uses one NTT in O(n log n). No accepted verdict was inferred from the blog.

The submission page visibly showed Accepted (1062 ms, 34412 KB), and the complete source was read. It implements threshold counts for v, r, and min(v,r), three NTT-based binomial transforms, and division by C(n,k), cross-checking the official threshold formulation.

Independently proved both the sorted-minima and threshold formulations, their equivalence, and the normalization by C(n,k). In-memory exhaustive checks covered all 66429 arrays of 1 <= n <= 5 ordered pairs (v_i,r_i) in {0,1,2}^2 and every k; direct subset enumeration always matched the threshold formula.

annotation.summary: For every subset size k, compute modulo 998244353 the average over all k-subsets S of E(S)=max(min_{i in S}v_i,min_{i in S}r_i).
annotation.constraints: Single test case; 1 <= n <= 200000; 0 <= v_i,r_i <= 1000000; output n modular averages under modulus 998244353; time limit 2 seconds; memory limit 512 MB.
annotation.core_idea: For a subset S, let A=min_S v, B=min_S r, and m_i=min(v_i,r_i). Then E(S)=max(A,B)=A+B-min(A,B)=min_S v+min_S r-min_S m. Sort v, r, and m independently in nonincreasing order. For any sorted array x_1>=...>=x_n, the total minimum over all k-subsets is sum_{i=k}^n x_i C(i-1,k-1), because choosing x_i as the minimum means selecting the other k-1 indices among 1..i-1. Therefore, with d_i=v_i^down+r_i^down-m_i^down, F(k)=sum_{|S|=k}E(S)=sum_{i=k}^n d_i C(i-1,k-1). Rewrite C(i-1,k-1)=(i-1)!/((k-1)!(i-k)!), reverse one coefficient array, and convolve d_i(i-1)! with inverse factorials by NTT. Finally output F(k)/C(n,k).
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) time for three sorts and one NTT convolution, O(n) auxiliary memory. The threshold-count variant uses O(V+n log n) time and O(V+n) memory for V=max(v_i,r_i)<=10^6.
tricks: Replace max of two subset minima by their sum minus the minimum of the pointwise minima.
Sort v, r, and min(v_i,r_i) independently; pairing is needed only before forming the third array.
Count a sorted value x_i as the subset minimum in exactly C(i-1,k-1) subsets.
Recognize the all-k factorial sum as a reversed convolution over modulus 998244353.
Divide the total over k-subsets by C(n,k) only after the transform.
uncertainty_points: The official slides present the same transform through threshold-difference coefficients f_i, while the primary variant here uses the equivalent direct sorted-minima identity because its indexing is easier to audit.
The accepted submission uses three threshold-count transforms rather than the one-convolution sorted formulation; both were proved equivalent and retained as separate solution variants.
No floating-point FFT is needed: modulus 998244353 supports an exact NTT. The existing repository tag math/transform/fft is reused according to current taxonomy convention.
No new taxonomy tag is proposed; existing minmax inclusion-exclusion, convolution, FFT, subset-counting, and sorting tags are sufficient.
proof_risks: min(min_S v,min_S r) equals min_{i in S}min(v_i,r_i); this equality is what permits the third independently sorted array.
The three arrays must be sorted independently. Sorting pairs once and reusing that order for all three minima is incorrect.
For descending order, x_i is the minimum only when index i is included and the other k-1 choices come from earlier indices, giving C(i-1,k-1), not C(i,k).
The factorial factor outside the convolution is 1/(k-1)! in the direct-minima formula; the threshold formulation using C(t,k) instead has 1/k!.
The reversed inverse-factorial array and convolution index must be checked carefully; a one-position shift swaps k with k-1.
In the threshold variant, C_e counts bloggers satisfying both v_i>=e and r_i>=e, equivalently min(v_i,r_i)>=e. It is not min(A_e,B_e).
The integer tail sum starts at e=1. Zero values contribute no threshold layer and require no special positive term.
The NTT length must cover the full convolution, and all negative coefficients from subtracting the min-array contribution must be normalized modulo 998244353.
The final answer is F(k)*C(n,k)^{-1}; omitting this normalization returns the sum, not the average.

solution_variants.name: sorted-minima-one-ntt
solution_variants.summary: Use max(A,B)=A+B-min(A,B), form the three arrays v, r, and min(v_i,r_i), sort each descending, combine their order statistics into d_i, and evaluate all sums sum_i d_i C(i-1,k-1) with one factorial convolution.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log n) time and O(n) auxiliary memory.

solution_variants.name: threshold-count-binomial-transforms
solution_variants.summary: Use E=sum_{e>=1}[E>=e]. For every threshold e, count A_e=#{v_i>=e}, B_e=#{r_i>=e}, and C_e=#{min(v_i,r_i)>=e}; then F(k)=sum_e(C(A_e,k)+C(B_e,k)-C(C_e,k)). Bucket equal counts and evaluate the resulting binomial transforms by NTT.
solution_variants.complexity: O(V+n log n) time and O(V+n) memory, where V=max(v_i,r_i)<=10^6.

tags.evidence: The central reduction is max(min_S v,min_S r)=min_S v+min_S r-min_S min(v_i,r_i), equivalently inclusion-exclusion of the two threshold events E>=e.
After extracting factorials, all n values F(k) are coefficients of one convolution between reversed d_i(i-1)! terms and inverse factorials; this removes the quadratic all-k summation.
The required convolution is evaluated by an NTT modulo 998244353, which is the decisive O(n log n) acceleration and follows the repository's existing FFT/NTT tag convention.
For descending x, exactly C(i-1,k-1) size-k subsets have x_i as their minimum, and C(n,k) is also the denominator of the requested average.
Sorting v, r, and min(v_i,r_i) independently converts arbitrary subset minima into order-statistic contributions indexed only by i.
